prd.md:392-393 pins invalid-explicit-tuple plus plausible-artifact refusal and one/zero/multiple artifact-candidate fixtures.

implementation_readiness.assessment: The corrected v16 blueprint is ready for autopilot-code: it names the projection owner and additive schemas, defines field-level evidence authority and ambiguity behavior, specifies the cross-harness child predicate, gives complete layout and DAG outcomes, and supplies hermetic completion tests. Readiness is a specification result; the current source remains the v15 baseline and is not claimed to implement v16.
ready_elements: One WorkProjection resolver and all group/process/plain/JSON consumers are named.
Exact route, registry-only identity, unique-cwd association, route-absent artifact, and ambiguity outcomes are testable.

OpenCode is normatively included in ordinary-child scheduling even though the current baseline refresher accepts only Claude/Codex; extending the read-only OpenCode DB/message adapter is correctly development work, not completed evidence.
Arbitrary composed DAG, parallel siblings, fan-in, width/overflow, additive JSON, numeric context decrease, TITLE size, quota, mirror, public-JSON, and zero-provider gates are explicit.
pipeline_state and pipeline_summary truthfully preserve specification done with development pending.

unsupported_runtime_contracts: The spec-read marker side effect could not write under /home/Uihyeop/agent_setting/.spec-grounding because that worker-visible location is read-only; the PRD itself was read completely.

No live default or custom title provider was invoked. Provider quotas, costs, and external behavior were intentionally not measured; the review used specification evidence and hermetic repository tests only.
A custom FLEET_TITLE_COMMAND wrapper's no-tools property cannot be enforced or introspected by Fleet beyond the shell-free argv contract; that property remains the wrapper owner's responsibility.

The selected baseline suite predates v16 implementation and cannot prove WorkProjection, the new all-layout detail row, unified child association, OpenCode refresher support, revised 3/4 quota behavior, or implementation-level zero-call acceptance until the subsequent autopilot-code cycle adds the specified fixtures.

The required post-write file-write heartbeat follows a completed test phase; the registry's monotonic phase contract rejects test-to-file-write as progress-phase-regression, so the durable artifact is finalized with the terminal artifact phase instead.
